perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information” means collectively and individually all or any information of whatsoever nature, di sclosed in any form, whether oral, visual, written or in electronic form (including, without limitation, any documentation, reports, calculations, processes, methodologies, manuals and membership data ) relating to [Company name] and FORS operators, audit processes and commercial data together with all records or copies or e xtracts thereof in whatever media held.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information” means collectively and individually only information explicitly marked as confidential in writing by the disclosing party at the time of disclosure. This excludes any information disclosed orally, visually, or electronically without a corresponding written designation.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad and inclusive. The modified definition restricts it to only information explicitly marked as confidential in writing, contradicting the original inclusive definition and creating uncertainty as to what is considered confidential. This is an In-Text Contradiction, the original definition is in SECTION 1, and now is being redefined to something completely different which impacts the understanding of the whole contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION “Confidential Information” means collectively and individually only information explicitly marked as confidential in writing by the disclosing party at the time of disclosure. This excludes any information disclosed orally, visually, or electronically without a corresponding written designation.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, including oral, visual, written, and electronic forms. The changed text restricts this definition to only information explicitly marked as confidential in writing. This directly contradicts the previous broad definition, as now, information disclosed orally or visually is explicitly excluded unless it has a corresponding written designation, creating a significant conflict. This contradicts the text in Section 1, which now only defines Confidential Information as explicitly marked confidential in writing, as per the 'Contradicted Text' provided.

perturbation.original_text: The above duties of confidentiality shall not apply to information that the receiving Party can demonstrate by reasonable documentary proof: - 2.2.1 to hav e been in the public domain at the time of receipt or to subsequently entered the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; 2.2.2 to have been l awfully known by the r eceiving P arty prior to its receipt; or 2.2.3 to have been disclosed to the receiving Party without restriction by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The above duties of confidentiality shall not apply to information that the receiving Party can demonstrate by reasonable documentary proof: - 2.2.1 to hav e been in the public domain at the time of receipt or to subsequently entered the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; 2.2.2 to have been l awfully known by the r eceiving P arty prior to its receipt; or 2.2.3 to have been disclosed to the receiving Party without restriction by a third party. This exception applies regardless of whether the information was explicitly marked confidential or not.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "This exception applies regardless of whether the information was explicitly marked confidential or not.", we create a contradiction. Section 1 now requires explicit written designation for information to be considered confidential. However, this new addendum in 2.2.3 suggests that even if information *was* explicitly marked confidential, if it falls under exceptions 2.2.1, 2.2.2, or 2.2.3, it's no longer confidential, effectively overriding the explicit written designation requirement and thus creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION “Confidential Information” means collectively and individually only information explicitly marked as confidential in writing by the disclosing party at the time of disclosure. This excludes any information disclosed orally, visually, or electronically without a corresponding written designation.
perturbation.justification: The original text, defining exceptions to confidentiality, is modified to include "This exception applies regardless of whether the information was explicitly marked confidential or not." This directly contradicts the "Confidential Information" definition, which states that information is only confidential if "explicitly marked as confidential in writing." The added text nullifies the explicit written designation requirement under specific exceptions, creating a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information: 3.1.1 only to such employees of the receiving Party as are strictly necessary for the Permitted Purpose; and/or 3.1.2 as required by any order of any court of competent jurisdiction or any competent judicial, governmental or regularly body.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information: 3.1.1 only to such employees of the receiving Party as are strictly necessary for the Permitted Purpose; and/or 3.1.2 as required by any order of any court of competent jurisdiction or any competent judicial, governmental or regularly body. The receiving party is allowed to disclose any information regardless of whether it is considered confidential or not.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause limits disclosure of 'Confidential Information' to specific circumstances. The added sentence, "The receiving party is allowed to disclose any information regardless of whether it is considered confidential or not," completely negates the restrictions outlined in 3.1.1 and 3.1.2, leading to a direct contradiction. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.  PERMITTED DISCLOSURE 3.1 The receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information: 3.1.1 only to such employees of the receiving Party as are strictly necessary for the Permitted Purpose; and/or 3.1.2 as required by any order of any court of competent jurisdiction or any competent judicial, governmental or regularly body. The receiving party is allowed to disclose any information regardless of whether it is considered confidential or not.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the original clause regarding the disclosure of confidential information. The original clause limits disclosure, while the added sentence removes all restrictions, creating a clear inconsistency in the document. The contradicted text and changed text are in the same location, where it clearly shows that the last sentence in the changed text invalidates the other clauses.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving Party shall notify anyone to whom it is authorised to disclose the Confidential Information that it is confidential and procure that they comply with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving Party shall notify anyone to whom it is authorised to disclose the Confidential Information that it is confidential and procure that they comply with this Agreement. However, the receiving party is not responsible if those individuals fail to maintain the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause places a responsibility on the receiving party to ensure that anyone they disclose 'Confidential Information' to complies with the agreement. The added sentence, "However, the receiving party is not responsible if those individuals fail to maintain the confidentiality of the information," directly contradicts this responsibility. It creates an in-text contradiction by imposing a duty and then immediately removing accountability for fulfilling it. This weakens the confidentiality protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.  THIRD PARTIES 4.1 The receiving Party shall notify anyone to whom it is authorised to disclose the Confidential Information that it is confidential and procure that they comply with this Agreement. On the request of the d isclosing Party, the r eceiving Party shall procure that they enter into a confidentiality agreement on terms and conditions no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement. However, the receiving party is not responsible if those individuals fail to maintain the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence that directly contradicts the original clause and the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text state the receiving party must 'procure' compliance with the agreement by those they disclose confidential information to. The changed text then adds that they are 'not responsible' if those individuals fail to maintain confidentiality, which directly undermines and contradicts the responsibility to 'procure' compliance. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If the disclosing Party so requests in writing, the r eceiving Party shall immediately: - 5.2.1 return all Confidential Information including copies to the disclosing Party ; and 5.2.2 destroy or permanently erase all copies made by the receiving Party and procure that anyone to whom the receiving Party ha s supplied copies destroys or permanently erases such copies and any further copies made by them.
perturbation.changed_text: If the disclosing Party so requests in writing, the r eceiving Party shall immediately: - 5.2.1 return all Confidential Information including copies to the disclosing Party ; and 5.2.2 destroy or permanently erase all copies made by the receiving Party and procure that anyone to whom the receiving Party ha s supplied copies destroys or permanently erases such copies and any further copies made by them. However, the receiving party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the complete return or destruction of 'Confidential Information' upon written request. The added sentence, "However, the receiving party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes," directly contradicts this requirement. It allows the receiving party to keep a copy, undermining the intent of complete return or destruction and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.  COPIES 5.2 If the disclosing Party so requests in writing, the r eceiving Party shall immediately: - 5.2.1 return all Confidential Information including copies to the disclosing Party ; and 5.2.2 destroy or permanently erase all copies made by the receiving Party and procure that anyone to whom the receiving Party ha s supplied copies destroys or permanently erases such copies and any further copies made by them. However, the receiving party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original clause 5.2 mandates the complete return or destruction of 'Confidential Information' including all copies. The changed text introduces an exception by allowing the receiving party to retain one copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the original requirement of complete return or destruction, as stated in both the changed text and the contradicted text. The explanation supports this, and clearly, retaining a copy contradicts a full return of all copies.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement comprises the full and complete agreement of the Parties her eto with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes and cancels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the P arties relating to the Confidential Information whether oral, express or implied.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement comprises the full and complete agreement of the Parties her eto with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes and cancels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the P arties relating to the Confidential Information whether oral, express or implied. However, any existing non-disclosure agreements between the parties concerning similar information shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that this agreement is the *complete* agreement and cancels all prior agreements. The added sentence, "However, any existing non-disclosure agreements between the parties concerning similar information shall remain in full force and effect," directly contradicts this. It allows prior agreements to remain valid, creating an in-text contradiction that creates uncertainty as to which agreement governs. This completely defeats the purpose of having a comprehensive agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.  GOVERNING LAW 7.2 This Agreement comprises the full and complete agreement of the Parties her eto with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes and cancels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the P arties relating to the Confidential Information whether oral, express or implied. However, any existing non-disclosure agreements between the parties concerning similar information shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence, "However, any existing non-disclosure agreements between the parties concerning similar information shall remain in full force and effect." The original text, and the contradicted text, explicitly states that this agreement "supersedes and cancels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties relating to the Confidential Information whether oral, express or implied." By allowing prior agreements to remain valid, the changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted sections, as it no longer comprises the full and complete agreement and does not supersede all prior agreements. This meets the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction, because the new clause directly conflicts with what the original/contradicted text clearly states.
